desc:
With now fallow farmland to the south, and an area of woodland which has
apparently been brutally and carelessly felled lying to the north, the road here
takes on a bleak aspect. Every slight gust of wind bites hard, with no nearby
hills or trees to reduce its force, and not a single weed manages to grow amid
the rocks and stones of the surface of the thoroughfare.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Travelling as it does now through a bleak and unsheltered part of the land, the
road takes on a windswept and unwelcoming aspect. Perhaps long ago it saw
frequent travellers, but now it has an air of disuse. Judging by all of the
rocks and stones which litter its surface, it seems unlikely that many
carriages, carts or wagons follow this route.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Sweeping from west to north, the road draws close to a small range of hills.
Hard by to the east is a sprawling forest, whose dark-limbed trees seem to reach
out toward the roadway. And to the south and northwest lie regions which appear
to have been hastily deforested at some time not too far into the past.
name: A Bend in the Road

desc:
The road changes course here, from a due east-west route, taking a turn to the
south for a time. A narrow band of hills rises above you to the north, offering
a ready place from which to watch people as they progress along the carriageway.
The lands to the west however offer no shelter or hiding place, for someone has
stripped them of the trees which once flourished there.
name: A Bend in the Road

desc:
The road winds east and west through the forest. A group of tall, dark trees
blocks the passage to the south, while the land opens slightly towards the hills
of the north.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road continues onward, its surface worn from many a traveller. The lands to
the north of the road rise quickly, blocking your view of the distant
Ettenmoors. To the south, the trees open slightly, offering access to a sombre
forest.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The Road begins to slope downwards toward the east where you spot a small stream
that crosses the path. The land to the south is thick with forest and
impassable, making the only option to stick to the road.
name: Old East Road

desc:
A winding waterway crosses right over the Old East Road here. It is brown and
muddy, but no more than half a foot deep. The road travels to the east and west
here, and the stream crosses the road from north to south.
name: Small Stream Crossing

desc:
The trail has almost been lost to the weeds growing north of you, and the
underbrush at the border of the forest that lies to the south. Luckily a few
large beasts must occasionally walk this path, as the earth has remained too
packed for even the toughest weeds.
name: Small Trail

desc:
North of you a large field of weeds and grasses can be seen, looking like a
tempting spot to rest and take a break. East the trail can be seen cutting its
way inbetween the two forests that have merged. It remains unknown what sinister
eyes may be watching from the forests, readying themselves for the unwary
traveller.
name: Small Trail

desc:
This small trail winds it's way through the forest here, following the southern
forest line to the west. The trees crouching in on the trail provide a few nice
shady spots to shelter from the hot summer sun, but they are sure to also hide a
few beasts of the forest.
name: Small Trail

desc:
Some sunlight can be seen creeping through the forest canopy, but even this
close to the border of the forest the light is filtered through many leaves,
producing a dusk-like environment even at the height of day.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Any sensible person would desire to go north at this point, where a small trail
might take them away from these woods and their black heart. Perhaps you are not
sensible, perhaps even foolhardy, in which case a southernly route can assure
you many more encounters with ferocious beasts.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The trees grow close here, as if to ward off those who might wish to wander
under their limbs. Their leaves are almost successful in warding off all the
light, and appear to become even more competent to the south, where darkness is
all that the eye can see.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Finding yourself deep within the woods, it has become easy to lose your way,
with the thick forest obscuring any landmarks that you might have used as a
reference. Finding your way out may be a matter of luck.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The thick forest here has replaced the sky with it's branches and leaves,
removing all light from above. Dark shapes and shadows flirt with the large
trees as you move around, keeping your eyes moving to and fro, but revealing
little of what lies ahead.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The forest is very efficient here, turning daytime into night beneath it's
leaves. It is also rather quiet, all the small animals having departed from the
vicinity long ago. The scent of evergreen is strong.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Deep and dark, the forest holds you in its grasp. Tangled boughs clutch at your
body and long finger-like twigs scratch and cling to exposed flesh. Strange
murmerings and creakings emanate from the dense undergrowth, lending the forest
a hostile and foreboding atmosphere.
name: Dense Black Woods

desc:
The damp, lichen covered boughs and trunks stand decades old, their leafy crowns
covered in dark green foliage. Insects scurry away from areas of disturbed bark,
scuttling back under cover as quickly as possible. The clammy, damp air catches
in your throat and the odour of dank wood fills the air.
name: Amid the Forest

desc:
A twisting and turning path is the best one can hope for in this dim and gloomy
woods. Any sense of direction is quickly lost amid the trees and everywhere you
look an indistinguishable trunk or branch or bough gazes back. The best you can
hope for is to chose a path and stick to it as best you can.
name: Lost in the Dim Forest

desc:
Thicker than the surrounding forest, a stand of firs grows dense and close
nearby. The trunks seem to form an almost impenetrable wall of wood and travel
through the forest is only possible following a tortuous route around the trees.
The cries of woodland birds can be heard from the foliage high overhead.
name: Dark Stand of Pinetrees

desc:
A deep blanket of silence lies over the woodland. No cries of bird or mammal
break the unnatural quiet. The silence emphasises the hostile aspect of the
crooked trees, standing sentinel over the vale. No respite from the forest is
obvious, the trees growing outward all around.
name: Dark and Silent Woodland

desc:
Sturdy firs rise thirty feet and more, their lofty upper branches waving in the
breeze high above you. The soft earth underfoot is laced with roots and vines,
growing down into the subsoil to suckle at the nutrients contained within. The
sweet smell of pine sap hangs in the air.
name: Pine Forest

desc:
A few trees stand solitary amid a sea of brush. The dense shrubs, ferns and
brambles grow to a height of several feet and form a uneven green surface over
which you know gaze. Several of the bushes sport long thorns which are coated
with an itchy, irritating moisture - a fact which becomes only too apparent as
the thorns catch skin and pierce flesh.
name: Sparse Woodland

desc:
Dense and bushy, the forest springs forth from the soft ground of the
trollshaws. The trees are surrounded by brush and undergrowth rising several
feet high. Occasional thorns and brambles pull and tug at exposed skin and tear
at clothing. Many small insects hover and skim over and through the undergrowth.
name: Overgrown Forest

desc:
Lit from above the high, thick branches of mixed cedar and oak cast angular
shadows on the lower forest. Shapes become difficult to distinguish in the
broken light providing perfect camouflage for any predatory creature abroad in
the woods. The land begins to rise slightly to what you guess to be the
south-west.
name: Shadowed Forest

desc:
To the east and south a thick forest rises out of the land, its hoary, old trees
dominating the landscape. But in this strip of land, just south of the road,
someone has laid the woodland to waste. All that remains of the trees are
charred and broken stumps, and the undergrowth has been cleared entirely. This
must have been some considerable time ago, however, for now many weeds, clumps
of thin grasses, and thick tufts of heather have begun to grow again.
name: Blighted Land at a Forest Border

desc:
A dry stone wall once divided this land from the road to the north, suggesting
that this was once part of a farm or smallholding. But now all that remains of
the structure are tumbled rocks and small boulders, overgrown with grass and
weeds. The size and number of rocks suggest that the wall was a tall one, and
the wide area over which they have been scattered give rumour to a violent and
malicious act of destruction.
name: Forgotten Farmland

desc:
Though this entire area covered by weeds, thin grass, and a few small bushes, it
is apparent that there was once a trail leading through here. Travelling from
the Old East Road to the north, the scattered rocks and stones show the path
which it must once have taken. Leading from the Old East Road to the north, they
make their way directly to some ruined buildings, not far to the south of here.
name: On a Rough and Broken Trail

desc:
The entangled branches overhead are so dark that it seems amazing that anything
can live here. A small opening can be seen far away to the east. The trees to
the south grow even closer together, and no light can penetrate their eerie
darkness.
name: Shadowed Forest

desc:
A barely discernible path twists its way to the south and east through the
entangled underbrush of this dark forest. An unaware traveller could easily get
stuck in the vines that cover the forest floor. The air is very heavy due to the
overhanging branches and the sheer amount of trees standing beside you.
name: Shadowed Forest

desc:
The trees grow close together here and a small line of trees leads to an opening
to the north. A small trail seems to be carved out of the trees, leading to
either east or west. The forest canopy looms far above the floor here, making it
possible to see the road to the north.
name: Shadowed Forest

desc:
Tall dark trees tower above the floor here seemingly unclimbable. The floor of
the forest is open and filled with plants and bushes. The trees close around the
outer edges of the clearing making all paths but the one to the west impassable.
name: A Small Clearing

desc:
The water here begins to clear and grow in depth and small boulders seem to grow
right out of the depths below. The shoulders on either side are steep and very
slippery. The water flows from north to south.
name: Small Stream

desc:
Small boulders rise out of the water creating ripples in the stream here. The
shoulders of the stream are lined with large boulders and the banks are quite
steep, but it should be possible to gain the shore.
name: Bend in the Stream

desc:
The water begins to shallow here and rocks can be seen amongst the bottom of the
winding stream. The forest to the west opens just enough to allow entry into the
darkness it conceals. The waterway turns east and south here.
name: Winding Stream

desc:
A small landing has been carved out of the surrounding trees giving easy access
to the stream that flows here. Some tree roots can be seen through the crystal
water of the stream keeping this forest richly nourished.
name: Shadowed Forest

desc:
The ground is littered with remains of animal bones and campfires. The hill
rises far enough to view the surrounding lands in all directions. The mountains
of the north dominate the horizon, whereas an endless plain flows south of the
road, seemingly melting its way into the sky.
name: Steep Bank

desc:
Only a small brown path can be seen here among the lush grass that surrounds the
road that turns here north and east. A hill rises to the south, which overlooks
the surrounding vegetation and seems an ideal place to rest.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
A few wagon marks are visible between the many horse tracks that lie in the
road's soft shoulder. A steep, muddy hill rises to the north, and the road
continues east and west.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The dirt path of the road here is wide and soft, with little vegetation at the
edges. A slippery rise to the north leads toward a steeper hillside. To the
south a thick clump of trees prevents your progress. The road itself travels
east and west from here.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The dirt here of the road is firm and dry, with little vegetation growing along
the banks of the turning trail. Trees start to populate the land to the east,
which leads to the Trollshaws. The road bends south and west here.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road bends from west to south, as it opens its way along a steep slope. Down
the hillside, not further than a league away to the southeast, a wide expanse of
flatland leads to the ford in the river Bruinen, the main pass from and towards
the Misty Mountains. On the west, the trail disappears into the shadows of a
narrow glen.
name: Bend in the Great East Road

desc:
The hills around meet here and a thin, winding path is formed by their joining.
The slopes rise too quickly to climb up, forcing the use of this path. A few
trees grow near the ridge, giving one another support when needed. A dense
growth of cedar trees loom above the hills to the east, blocking the horizon.
name: Narrow Ridge

desc:
The earth is littered with corpses and animal remains, leaving ants and flies a
feast unlike any other. The stench is horrid and almost unbearable, the only
exit is the narrow pass among the hills to the west.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Tracks become more sparse as the winding trail slims to a few yards wide. A dark
group of trees blocks passage to the west, but to the south the land opens out
into a vast plain. Turning from north to east, the road travels onward across
the countryside.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The road opens wide here, and little blocks your immediate view of the lands
around you. The Trollshaws begin to consume the land to the north. A green
meadow fills the southern horizon, and the road goes east and west here.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Straight and narrow, the road continues to plough through the countryside to the
east and west here. A grassy green meadow grows to the south, filled with a
variety of plants that contrast in colour to the brown path of the road.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Straight as an arrow, the road stretches off into the distance to both the west
and east. Its origins are not clear, but it may have been built when the Third
Age of Arda was still young. By that time, the men had established the
North-kingdom in Eriador, and the Old Road would greatly help them travel
between their settlements.
name: Great East Road

desc:
The old, dusty road continues to cut through the uneven landscape of the ancient
realm of Rhudaur. The trail seems to run straight for the next few miles,
opening into a small glen a bit further eastwards. Although well-worn indeed, it
is still the easiest path through these inhospitable lands. It is therefore
common to find marks of both travellers and steeds on its surface.
name: Great East Road

desc:
The road continues to lead straight, as if ignoring the uneven terrain all
around. No more than a mile further eastwards, it opens into a deep ravine with
rocky, reddish walls. They stand so close to each other that one could think
that the gully was slit open by the mighty blow of a giant sword. But even here,
the rockfaces on both sides tower high above the road, leaving no other option
than to progress along the path.
name: Great East Road West of a Gully

desc:
The road's width suddenly narrows as it runs through a deep cutting. Towering
pines perched over moist walls of red stone cast shadows on the diminished path.
The sheer, cliff-like rock faces rise upright, making any attempt to climb them
pointless. The light rustle of a falling rock, or the faint patter of tiny feet
echo through the shadowy, tunnel-like passage.
name: Great East Road through a Rocky Gully

desc:
Large pine saplings have managed to spring up amid the rocky soil here. Their
tender boughs partially obscure the sky. To the north and west stand tall,
thick-boled trees, growing so densely they prevent all light from penetrating
their canopy. Many of the saplings have their lower limbs broken off, as though
some large creature had forced its way through here recently. To the south, the
Great East Road continues its endless way through Eriador.
name: Lightly-wooded Roadside

desc:
From this narrow ledge, half-way down the rocky wall of the forested plateau,
you can see the Old Road heading into a deep cutting, only one or two miles away
to the east. Below the projecting perch, the rock face drops vertically,
preventing any attempt to descend down to the road. But one can climb up to the
top, or progress along the cliff, making use of the many footholds.
name: A Precarious Perch

desc:
A wide, rocky platform reaches out from atop this cliff of reddish stone, as if
hanging on the air. No other spot nearby can offer such a view. At the foot of
the rock face, many feet below, the Great East Road cuts its way through the
narrow glen. But a bit further eastwards, it suddenly emerges out of the high,
imprisoning walls, and after a steep descent, it finally reaches the ford in the
river Bruinen, and then disappears into the foothills beyond.
name: Vantage Point over the Road

desc:
Even at the riverside, the terrain looks quite rugged. The Bruinen flows along
the bottom of an abrupt dale, and the ground ascends steeply as it leaves the
waters behind. Parallel to the course of the stream, a blurred track leads in a
north-south direction, zigzagging through the projecting rocks and clumps of
trees, to meet the Great East Road.
name: An Old Trail along the Riverbank

desc:
The current accelerates notably, and the depth of the river lessens, as it runs
closer to a fordable area clearly visible only a couple miles southwards. A
small eddy here and there hints about the presence of large, treacherous
boulders underwater. The river flows confined between two hillocks with sheer
hillsides, their wet, rocky faces plummeting down into the waters.
name: Bruinen River Approaching a Ford

desc:
The descending stream bends once again sharply to the south. Here it seems to
hasten shyly, almost imperceptibly, as if anticipating something. When the
beasts are quiet and weather is mild, and silence reigns in the valley, one can
hear the soft rippling of the flowing waters. And it sounds like a faint
whisper, as though the murmuring river recalled tales of ancient battles waged
in these lands in a long-forgotten past.
name: A Meander in the Bruinen River

desc:
Protruding rock and fallen trees mark the end of the dark, gloomy forest that
lies to the north. A thick bed of brownish pine needles carpets the floor, but
here and there moist rocks show up among the undergrowth, giving hint of the
rocky ground that must lie underneath. The wood comes to an end to the south,
where it meets a road leading in an east-west direction.
name: Outskirts of the Trollshaws

desc:
The wide variety of trees in this part of the forest seem to grow out of one
another, blocking passage in most directions. A large mound of animal remains
lies on the ground at your feet.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
The tall cedar trees grow more densely around this part of the forest path, and
a towering wall of pines looms above you at the southern extreme of the
woodland. The only apparent exit from this area seems to be to the north.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
The path from the road begins to fade and the forest quickly engulfs you with
its power. Tall trees of numerous varieties grow at first well apart, then
seemingly on top of one another. What light that the south and west offer stops
immediately at a group of fir trees to the north.
name: Edge of the Trollshaws

desc:
Fir trees claim this part of the forest for their own, their needles cover the
forest floor. Remains of animals sit piled in a small mound near a group of
trees to the east. The Trollshaws begin to separate to the south and a hint of
light can be seen there.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
This dismal forest seems to have grown deliberately for the creatures for which
it is named: the trolls. Not even the dimmest ray of light opens its way through
the black canopy of the trees. Below them, only an emaciated, brown,
sickly-looking undergrowth seems to sprout among huge stones projecting from the
ground. A strong, piercing smell impregnates the air.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
A dreary growth of fir trees blocks the passage eastwards, though the other
parts of the forest are clear enough to pass. A thick stench rises from the
gathered heap of corpses, animal remains, and rotting bones that lie here.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
As one steps into the deepest parts of the forest, it only becomes stranger and
stranger. At times, you find lofty trees bent like aged men, as if trying to
conceal with their leaves any small chink in the dark canopy. At other times,
you encounter huge trunks lying on the ground, as though torn from the earth for
some unknown purpose.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Rows of fir trees soon block any hope of going north through the forest. The
ground here is hard, dried, and littered with dead or dying vines and leaves.
The forest continues to loom over the ground to the east and south, and an
intersection of trees can be seen to the west.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
The enormous trees that grow here seem to tear the earth apart as they force
their way skywards. They are so tall that their tops are entirely hidden from
view amongst the deep shadows. The scrubby plant-life growing at the fringes of
the forest quickly dies out as the gloom deepens. To the east, the forest
appears to become lighter.
name: Towering Pines

desc:
Lofty and straight stand the trunks of the trees in this area of the forest,
like carefully arranged living pillars. And in the heights, their dark tops,
shrouded in black shadows, form an unreachable canopy of leaves. But down here
pale ferns and prickly vines grow plunged into the darkness, springing out from
the thick layer of needles and moss that carpets the stony ground.
name: Dense Pine Forest

desc:
You are now in the corner of a high plateau. This is an abrupt area indeed, and
any attempt to progress demands considerable effort. To the south, it is
possible to climb down to a narrow perch that hangs above a deep hollow, but not
further. Conversely, a gravelly embankment drops down along the east face, but a
small ridge leans against the cliff, making the descent shorter and easier.
Large heather grow among the rocks, and so do a few lonely trees wherever the
boulders leave enough room for their roots.
name: Scattered Pines atop an Embankment

desc:
A small pinnacle of stone leans against the greyish wall that rises in the west,
as if it had fallen during a fierce storm, or as though it had been pushed down
by a giant among giants, and turned into a stair of stone. From atop the ridge
it is possible to find a route to the summit of the cliff. Turning to the east
the lands descend abruptly towards the river, partially seen behind the rocky
crags.
name: At the Foot of an Embankment

desc:
A sheer wall of bare, grey rock plummets down into the rocky and rather barren
lands below. The slope is too steep to attempt any descent here. Nevertheless,
the edge of the high plain stretches away to the north and south, and it may be
possible to find a way down a bit further. Huge pine trees stand close to the
west, on the fringes of a wide forest. However, they bear a somewhat awkward
appearance.
name: Border of a Forested Plateau

desc:
A cliff of cold, grey stone towers imposingly on the west, preventing any
attempt to move in that direction. Only a few stunted, dried trees perch atop
it, their boughs mostly devoid of leaves because of the wind. Nights come fast
in the shadow of the great rock wall. But the rocky ground is often covered in
hoarfrost when the first rays of light begin to shine over the summits of the
Misty Mountains.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
A narrow track flanks the river, following its course. The path is merely a
diffused line as a result of the pass of travellers and beasts, rather than a
deliberately laid road. A group of trees with half-bare branches grow beside the
path here and there, but the ground is mostly covered in thicket. To the north,
the earth becomes more barren, vegetation is scarce, and the trail fades away.
name: Old Trail

desc:
This ample extension of land is surrounded by the calm stream that takes a
gentle turn here from north to west. The bank looks certainly fertile, perhaps
due to the fact that it is bathed by the pure waters of the Bruinen. Grass
sprouts thick beside the water, but the further you move away from the flow,
vegetation grows more and more scarcely. Even though, there are little pools
littered about in the plains, maybe a sign that the river once had a different
course through the flatland.
name: Grassy Riverbank

desc:
After the turbulent meeting of both forks, the river slowly recovers its quiet
appearance, and goes on with its long course with redoubled strength. As the
riverbed widens to accommodate the renewed stream, it also cuts its way deeper
in the ground. The water looks quite clear again, and it runs down calmly, yet
without a break, as if aware of the countless miles still awaiting ahead.
name: A Bend in the River Bruinen

desc:
A high, cliff-like wall of dark grey stone rises all along the eastern bank of
the river, as it winds its way through the moors of this rugged land. The
waters, though limpid, are shrouded in the thick shadows cast by the rocky wall,
and thus show rather dark shades usually. Every now and then, a small rock flies
off from the stony face, dropping noisily into the calm waters, and rising small
ring-shaped waves that quickly fade.
name: Bend in the Bruinen

desc:
This small and lonely hill rises above the height of the forests, which curve
around its base to the north and to the west. Eastward the land slopes gently
down into a narrow ribbon of low, rolling hills, which border the Old East Road.
East and west, the road cuts like a scar across the land, just to the south of
here, and this prominence offers you a commanding view of all who travel by this
route.
name: Atop a Small Hill

desc:
Hemmed in, between a small hill to the south, and a sprawling stream to the
north, this part of the forest is characterised by its muddy ground. But, though
comparatively little undergrowth manages to survive in the soggy earth, the
trees here are surprisingly tall and form a dense, almost impenetrable canopy
overhead. Perhaps their roots, digging deeper into the ground than those of the
smaller plants, are able to find less choking conditions.
name: In a Dark and Gloomy Forest

desc:
A small stream makes its way through the woods just north of here, and its
waters must often breach their low banks, for the land is quite muddy. Little
undergrowth manages to survive in this swampy soil, but the trees loom high
above you, forming a dense canopy overhead. Little or no natural light can ever
reach this place, and because of this the area is pervaded by the smell of death
and decay.
name: In a Dark, Decaying Forest

desc:
Death and decay besmirch the air in this part of the forest. A small stream
flows idly by, just to the northeast, and its lazy waters spread out across the
shore, rotting any deadwood, and choking the undergrowth. only the trees seem
able to survive, perhaps because their roots dig so much deeper. And with no
competition from other plants, they have thrived, growing tall and dense, their
boughs knotting together to form a dense canopy through which no natural light
can pass.
name: Dark and Dreary Woodland

desc:
The waters of the small stream which meanders past here to the north must often
break their banks, for the land here is rather boggy. Deadwood from the trees
has rotted badly upon the ground, and few bushes and weeds manage to grow. Yet
the trees themselves are tall and dense, perhaps because their roots dig that
much deeper. The result is that they have thrived, and their long limbs stretch
out overhead, blotting out any view of the sky above.
name: Amid a Dark, Dense Forest

desc:
Nestled against the side of a hill to the east, the forest seems to be rather
dense here, yet ends abruptly just to the south. The dividing line seems quite
unnaturally regular, as if someone had cleared the lands between here and the
road. Little undergrowth is to be seen, and indeed it might be that someone has
also cut that away. But the trees themselves grow tall and strong, their long
limbs knitting close together, forming an dense canopy overhead, through which
no natural light seems able to penetrate.
name: At the Eaves of a Dark Forest

desc:
Though the stream flows continually through this area, its waters are quite
choked with fallen boughs and other deadwood, which has partially rotted. This
pollutes the air with the distinctive odour of decay, lending an unwholesome
aspect to the waterway. Thick trees lean over the brook from both of its muddy
banks, casting a pall on even the brightest of sunny days or moonlit nights.
name: A Meandering Waterway

desc:
Tall trees, wide in girth and great in age stand all about you, their heavy
boughs knitting together to form an impenetrable canopy, through which almost no
natural light can filter. And because of this, and because of the muddy ground
underfoot, almost no undergrowth is to be seen in this part of the forest. A few
twisted weeds, and an occasional thin, spindly bush are all that grows at ground
level, and much of this is tainted by rot and mould.
name: In a Dark and Brooding Woodland

desc:
The stream here has a very slight current and is crystal clear. The bank of the
water is lined with small stones of diverse variety. The river continues its
winding journey through the forest to the southeast.
name: Small Stream

desc:
The bank of the water on all sides is rocky and steep, making it impossible to
climb. The stream bends here heading south along base of the hills. Soon the
turns come too often to see what the next one will bring.
name: Bend in the Stream

desc:
The bank of the water to the west is lined with rocks, yet can be easily climbed
to view the lush meadow that lies there. The stream continues its eastward trek
to the Trollshaws in the east.
name: Small Stream

desc:
The water here flows among many stones and broken trees, making ripples in the
normally flat surface. The stream opens slightly in the east to a valley below
the hills and continues south along the Trollshaws.
name: Bend in the Stream

desc:
The water here quickly becomes deeper and somehow darker. The forest slowly
encroaches the edges of the waterway, and soon all that surrounds you is the
forest known as the Trollshaws. The water continues east here, deeper into the
darkness. The water flows from the north out of the hills above the forest.
name: Small Stream in the Trollshaws

desc:
The forest around the stream is so dark that the water is barely visible below
you. The trees slowly open to the east, and it is possible to climb the hill
there that looms above the Trollshaws. The stream turns from west to south here.
name: Small Stream in the Trollshaws

desc:
The hill here is quite barren of life except for the grass at your feet. The
hill rises high like the hills to the west and gives a clear view to the land to
the south and the stream that intersects the road in the east.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
The land steadily rises to a tall hill here giving a clear view of the Road.
Small bushes and trees grow in abundance on this knoll, but it is hard to avoid
gazing at the mountains of the north that claim the horizon for their own
majesty.
name: Rise along the Stream

desc:
Grass grows in abundance along the twisting stream. There are few bushes or
trees near, but soon the vegetation becomes more dense. A soft shoulder along
the stream makes it easy to reach the crystal waters that flow here.
name: Meadow along the Stream

desc:
Turning here from north to east, the stream continues to meander through the
dense woodland. Tall trees loom high above you, their branches leaning out part
way across the water, casting a gloomy pall on the area, and allowing all manner
of insects to thrive. Despite the gentle current of the waterway, the
near-constant shadows have hastened decay, and a sickly odour of rotting wood
seems to pervade the area.
name: A Meandering Waterway

desc:
The stream ambles lazily between the trees which overshadow its banks. Turning
here from the west to the south, it passes through some particularly gloomy
parts of the forest, where the canopy is so dense as to eliminate any natural
light. Thus, even the waterway is cast in gloom for most of the day.
name: A Meandering Waterway

desc:
High and dark the forest grows over, above and around you. The trunks of the
trees seem almost black in the dim light, their branches seem to descend and
grasp at and tangle with your head and body. The rustling of branches and leaves
rises and falls though no wind can be felt under the canopy.
name: Deep in the Dark Woods

desc:
The land seems lower here, though the thick forest all around makes the gauging
of direction or distance difficult. The trees crowd you, their low hanging
branches knocking and scratching at heads. In all direction the dark forest
continues, its dark canopy overshadowing the black earth.
name: Deep in the Darkened Forest

desc:
Long, slender pines let the light travel between their branches. The smell of an
old forest fills your nostrils and relaxes you. There is a large hill some
distance to the northeast.
name: In the Forest

desc:
The land around you begins to rise slowly to a stony hill above the forest. A
few clumps of trees sprout out alongside the mound. The landscape is dominated
by the mountains to the north, whereas a dark forest governs the region to the
east.
name: Mound above the Forest

desc:
A slight path can be seen here, worn from travellers far and wide coming here to
view the spectacular scenery. Ashes of long gone campfires remain in contrast to
the greenery that surrounds them. The land begins to descend to the east here,
whereas the hills remain high to the south and west.
name: Mound above the Forest

desc:
Rocks seem to be growing out of the earth all around you. A huge pile of large
boulders dominates the northern end of the hill. The huge grey stones, placed in
no particular order, seem awkward and dangerously close to falling over the edge
into the valley below.
name: On the Hills

desc:
The ground here is soft from the flowing waters to the west. The descent down to
the water is clear and easily made. A group of large, dark trees blocks the
passage to the east, the Trollshaws have begun.
name: Rise above the Stream

desc:
The waters of the gentle stream spread wide here, making the ground to either
side somewhat marshy. Sedges and bullrushes abound, and the area makes an ideal
habitat for all manner of water-loving insects, since the tall trees of the
surrounding woodland leave it in deep shade for much of the day.
name: A Spreading Stream

desc:
It was no act of nature which caused the flourishing forest to the north to end
so abruptly, and at such a distance from the road. Clearly the woodland was
visited by an act of deliberate destruction. The undergrowth has been burned
away completely, to leave not a single bush or shrub above about six inches in
height. And every tree that was too large simply to burn down has been felled by
axe or saw, then its timbers removed or destroyed, leaving behind only the
ragged stumps.
name: A Ribbon of Clear-cut Land

desc:
Where once there stood oaks and elms, tall pines and firs, now remain only a
host of charred and broken stumps. Even the undergrowth which must once have
flourished here has been destroyed. But all of this destruction must have
happened long ago, for now a few thin patches of grass, and some tiny clumps of
weeds have begun to take hold, and the area is starting to return to life once
more.
name: The Broken Remains of a Forest

desc:
The forest stretches out to the north, to the east and to the west, but to the
south it ends rather abruptly as if cut down by someone. Further to the north
the woods are characterised by rather swampy terrain, which strangles all the
undergrowth, but here the forest seems quite a natural and airy one, with a
variety of bushes, shrubs and flowers thriving amid the tall trees.
name: At the Eaves of a Dense Forest

desc:
The ground is sodden underfoot. Water wells up around feet and hooves as they
press down into the soggy vegetation. Many bushes grow thickly to a height of
three or four feet. A few trees grow amid the brush but they seem stunted by the
excess water. Many biting insects flourish in the damp conditions and once
disturbed, follow your movements through the undergrowth.
name: Soggy Undergrowth

desc:
A small lake to the west ends underfoot here in a soggy morass of rushes, mud
and weeds. Floundering through this marshland is hard going indeed as the mud
sucks at feet and hooves making each step an effort. To the north, south and
east woodland surrounds the marsh, indicating that firmer ground may lie in
those directions.
name: Deep Marshland

desc:
The forest floor is damp and sodden underfoot. With the trees sucking moisture
from the ground, some water source must be present nearby to maintain the soil
in this state. A moist brown odour rises from the forest floor, most likely dead
vegetation rotting in the soggy conditions.
name: Damp Forest

desc:
The upper branches of the woodland grow together to form a green canopy over the
forest floor. Here and there a chink in the foliage allows light to filter from
above. The rich smell of moist earth and wood lingers in the still air of the
forest. To the west, the ground seems to rise slightly.
name: Beneath a Canopy of Boughs

desc:
The thick foliage of the woodland cuts down any light from the skies above to a
mere glimmer amid the permanent dusk. The choking of the light prevents the
growth of underbrush and the forest floor is a uniform gray-brown carpet of
decaying leaves and branches. The gloom extends around you in all directions,
deepening to black as the trees close in.
name: Dim Dark Forest

desc:
Dark fir trees grow tall from the moist black earth. Their aspect is singular
and foreboding. The air of the forest seems heavy with suspense, though no
immediate danger seems obvious. Only a few furtive rustles and cracks break the
dead silence of the woods.
name: Dark and Sombre Pines

desc:
The network of branches above your head thickens to the extent that little light
can pass through its dark leafy canopy. The trees themselves are twisted and
misshapen. The forest has a tainted aura which seems to have infected the flora
and fauna with its insidious grasp. Unrecognisable harsh cries sounds out
occasionally, breaking the ominous silence.
name: Black and Dismal Forest

desc:
The bare northern face of this mound gazes out over the forest of the
trollshaws. The land slopes down to the north, east and west where the trees
begin nigh a hundred feet from your present position. To the south, you could
scramble up the slope which looks to climb another few hundred feet to the low
summit.
name: In the Lee of a Hill

desc:
Some cataclysmic occurrence ripped the stones apart here. The ground is rutted
and defiled by the shattered corpses of ancient stones. It appears that Morgoth
himself walked through here wearing very heavy boots.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
You are standing on a rocky hill which you climbed by means of an old rough
trail. The exertion has rendered you breathless but the sweat chills on your
skin when you sense the aura of total evil that surrounds this place. Before you
stands an old stone fortress, squat and sinister.
name: Rock Hill

desc:
Again the sudden change takes you by surprise. The small disused trail has
become an old road which arrows northward. The raised surface, the larger than
normal width and the incredible straightness mark this as a military road. It
appears to have been abandoned centuries ago yet still the surface holds good.
To the west lies a rocky plain.
name: Ancient Road

desc:
All around you lie the remains of what was once an extensive military outpost.
Long ago some great army marched here and you see the old decayed signs of their
presence. The air is charged with evil and the walls seem to ring with the
cadence of a long forgotten march. There is an old inscription on the wall and
stairs leads upward.
name: Fortress

desc:
The room is dark and old and damp. You can hear the ancient stones groan as you
move amongst them. The air feels chilled, and a slight draught disturbs it from
time to time. On the walls you can make out some ancient carvings, but their
substance has all but faded to nothing.
name: Beneath the Fortress

desc:
Here the whole region is visible, except for where in the south east the large
mass of rock obscures the view. To the north the old road runs straight as an
arrow towards Ettenmoors and distant Angmar.
name: On the Battlement

desc:
The trail turns northward here in an abrupt and unexpected fashion. All a around
you there is a wasteland of shattered rock and barren plain. To the west you can
see a rocky hill on which there appears to be some old fortifications. It is
almost as if the trail recoils from this place, fleeing north and east at the
first available opportunity.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail curves southward into the fringes of the forest and descends gently
among the trees.
name: Trail through the Trees

desc:
The trail bends a little to avoid the lonely pinnacle of rock which stands like
a guardian at the forest's edge. No trees or large shrubs grow within twenty
feet of the base.
name: Trail

desc:
You hear the tinkle of running water to the south and heave a sigh of relief
that the path has widened a little. The trail continues east-west and to the
west you can see a tall crag standing like a sentinel at the forest edge.
name: Trail

desc:
Along the edge of a deep canyon, the path wends its way, and clearly there is no
way to travel to the north of the trail due to this obstruction. To the south
however, you might choose to make your way into the rolling hills.
name: Trail

desc:
The path here is extremely narrow, as it works its faltering way along the lip
of a deep and treacherous canyon to the north; clearly there is no way to travel
in that direction. To the south, however, stands a line of hills.
name: Trail

desc:
Here the trail plods steadily from east to west across the land. At some
distance to the south stands an old building. But even at this distance it is
apparent that it has long been abandoned.
name: Trail

desc:
A narrow trail cuts its way across the land here, proceeding almost perfectly
east to west, with scarcely any diversion from its path. To the south of the
trail stands an old yard, whilst to the north the way is blocked by a deep and
dangerous canyon - clearly it would be impossible to travel that way.
name: Trail

desc:
You are standing on a trail running from the east to the west. To the north is
the edge of a vast and deep canyon. To the south, a huge forest dominates the
landscape, rising high into the sky. The sight of the forest causes a bit of
unease in your mind.
name: Canyon Trail

desc:
You are standing on a trail that follows a huge canyon to the north. Though
stable here, the trail quickly turns into a steep drop to the north and east. To
the southwest and far south, a large forest rises high into the sky. This trail
leads somewhere, but following it might require more than simply walking.
name: Canyon Trail

desc:
Bending again around the canyon's rim, the trail leads you onwards to the
unknown. A tall, impenetrable wall of trees blocks the south and west. The size
of the forest piques your curiosity, but the sinister appearance daunts your
courage. All that matters now is to avoid falling off the trail.
name: Canyon Trail

desc:
Running parallel to the deep canyon to the north, the trail leads you along to
your destination, known or unknown. The tall trees to your south, though draw
your attention more and more. Your pace slows as you contemplate the dangers of
exploration, but it will have to wait until you can glimpse a way in.
name: Canyon Trail

desc:
Travelling along the only obvious path here, you follow your adventuresome
spirit. To remind you of your mortality, the depths of the canyon and the
towering trees mock whatever confidence you once had. Though it would be foolish
to attempt to go north, a way into the forest might be found eventually.
name: Canyon Trail

desc:
In the north, the canyon seems to rise into the rest of the landscape to the
northeast. The trail itself bends to the south, entering part of the forest as
it does so. Your choices might not include staying out of the forest if you move
south.
name: Canyon Trail

desc:
Standing at this part of the trail, the tall trees form a canopy over your head,
blocking out most of the light from above. This part of the forest is rather
peaceful, but the thick and dark trees to the south indicate that not all of the
forest is of such silvan character.
name: Trail through a Forest

desc:
The trail narrows here, confined by the walls of trees lining both the north and
south sides. To the north, though, a small path is discernible in between
massive trunks. To the south, you believe you could slip into the dark depths,
but the inherent dangers of such a place force you to reconsider. The canopy of
trees even here is sufficient to block out enough light to put this place into
dim twilight.
name: Trail through a Forest

desc:
At this point, no light escapes the thick green ceiling of tall branches and
large leaves. Though smaller than before, the trail stubbornly moves onward
defying the aggression of the trees. To the south, the forest is dark and
impenetrable, curbing your adventuresome spirit. The trees to the north, though
thick, leave sufficient room for you to explore there.
name: Trail through a Forest

desc:
Thin beams of light illuminate the trail, stubbornly working their way through
the green ceiling above. Thick trees prevent any access into the forest around
you, leaving you no choice but to remain on the trail. You console your spirit
with the cold knowledge that ignorance can be at least healthy, if not blissful.
name: Trail through a Forest

desc:
En route to the ground far below, your mind leaves your body, taking the time to
admire the landscape in the few seconds before impact. Maybe the next
opportunity, you will choose to respect the physical laws which govern Arda.
name: Canyon

desc:
En route to the ground far below, your mind leaves your body, taking the time to
admire the landscape in the few seconds before impact. Maybe the next
opportunity, you will choose to respect the physical laws which govern Arda.
name: Canyon

desc:
The ground here has been shattered and broken by some titanic force. There are
no living creatures, no plants, no water. The splintered rocks lie like the
discarded eggshells from some gigantic omelette. The recipe appears to have been
a failure. To the south you can see a shattered mountain whose unclimbable scree
strewn slopes rear steeply above the forest.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
Here the devastation is complete. Tortured shapes and twisted forms litter the
ground. Polished skulls, stripped clean of flesh by carrion crows and other
vermin, lie discarded amongst the detritus of battle. Rusted weapons, shattered
sword hilts and broken shields leave no doubt as to what occurred here ages
past.
name: Battlefield

desc:
The forest peters out here, confronted on the north by a trail, the east by a
blasted plain and the west by a spire of rock. To the south the trees thicken
rapidly, but here they grow almost unwillingly.
name: Light Forest

desc:
You are in a wasteland of broken rock. South and east the forest lies, patiently
advancing northward. North of here you see an old trail all around you is
devastation and ruin.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
Held between the shattered arms of the broken mountain the plain looks north and
east. The south and west are impassable scree covered slopes, the north and east
are barren wastelands.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
A tangle of stony ridges juts out from the plateau to the east. The ground
generally slopes downward toward forested vales to the north and west, but deep
ravines cut at seemingly random angles carve the ridges apart into great stony
blocks. Low cliffs and sinkholes are everywhere. The rotten limestone is barely
covered by scraggly bushes and a few ancient, twisted firs. There is no sign of
civilisation.
name: Stony Outcrops

desc:
The forested land drops sharply toward the west in a series of escarpments cut
through with gullies and choked with rotten piles of collapsed stone. Spruces
and firs jut from the slope at strange angles, greedily twisting toward the cold
light of the sun. There is no water in the ravines, only dank moss and talus.
Here and there are some harder blocks of stone which seem to have been worked by
tools; but there is no obvious sense of what they used to be.
name: Rough Slope

desc:
The forest is sparse here, as the land rises slowly from the plateau to the
west, becomes stony and barren, then plummets down to the west in a tangle of
gullies and collapsed blocks of stone. The soil is thin here, and the rotten
limestone underneath, cut through with joints and crevices, is exposed on every
slope. The conifers are dwarfed, beaten down, twisted.
name: Edge of the Plateau

desc:
You are in a dark, dense forest. All sounds of animals and the like have
dissappeared. The light has grown very dim. Strange vegetation crowds about you.
It has an unfamiliar odour and you find yourself becoming disoriented.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
You are in a dark, dense forest. All sounds of animals and the like have
dissappeared. The light has grown very dim. Strange vegetation crowds about you.
It has an unfamiliar odour and you find yourself becoming disoriented.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
You are in a dark, dense forest. All sounds of animals and the like have
disappeared. No sunlight can reach the floor. Strange vegetation crowds about
you. It has an unfamiliar odour and you find yourself becoming disoriented.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
You are in a very dense forest. All sounds of animals and the like have
disappeared. The light has grown very dim. Strange vegetation crowds about you.
It has an unfamiliar odour and you find yourself becoming disoriented.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
A steep-sided ravine slashes through the old rotten hills here. There is no
water flowing in it; water drips from its sides, but is lost in the water-worn
joints and crevices of the limestone. The floor of the ravine is covered with
deep ferns on a surface of loose rubble. The ravine trends toward the southwest
and plunges steeply downward. The wall of the ravine's south side is so steep
that its floor must lie in perpetual shadow.
name: Dark Ravine

desc:
Rough, steep-sided hillocks rise up here from the uneven forest floor, raggedly
covered with low evergreen brush and crowned by clumps of twisted old pines. The
rock of which they are formed is rotten, spongy limestone, honeycombed with
crevices and gaping joints. The ground underfoot is full of potholes and stone
blocks.
name: Hillocks

desc:
Rising a hundred feet or so above the surrounding ground, the top of this small
hillock looks out over the forests of the trollshaws. All around the ground
drops away into forest though the north face of the hillock is more barren than
the others, perhaps due to the lack of sun on its aspect. Underfoot the grass
lies flattened by the wind scouring the top of the rise.
name: Small Hilltop

desc:
Many types of tree grow together in a jumble of shape and colour. All around
firs, cedar, oak and birch compete for ground and light. The trees shade much of
the light from the ground and the forest floor lives in perpetual shade. Here
and there a rabbit burrow gapes up blackly from the soft moist earth. Piles of
small droppings suggest that the burrows have many inhabitants.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Here the trees of the woodland are spaced wide and thin. Much shrub and brush
cover the gaps between the firs in an untidy jumble of shapes and size. The area
has an unkempt look to it as if nature herself has lost interest in the doleful
place. A peculiar rotting odour hangs in the air, smearing itself across the
back of your throat.
name: Dismal Woodland

desc:
The coarse ground of the forest floor climbs upward to the north, the trees
appearing to lean over on the slope as they reach skyward. As the slope rises,
the tree cover thins and the bare crest of the hillock can be seen some way off
to the north. To the south, east and west the forest thickens as it descends to
the lowlands of the trollshaws.
name: Wooded Draw

desc:
Thick, dank forest, perpetually dark, grows here in a vale surrounded on the
north and east sides by steep limestone escarpments. The stony ground is covered
with deep moss, ferns, and fungus, and the air is damp and musty. Odd squawks
and rustling sounds come from the dark boughs overhead.
name: Forested Vale

desc:
Crumbling limestone bluffs topped with unhealthy-looking conifers face west and
overlook the dense forest below. There are no good trails up the escarpment,
though there are a few hand-holds by means of which one might scramble down, if
one wants to risk putting one's weight on rock which has been rotting for a
thousand years. There are some chunks of granite which might once have been a
fortification, in the days when men of the Dunedain ruled here.
name: Difficult Slope

desc:
The forest has grown suddenly dark and oppressive. There are no animal sounds
just the cracking of twigs beneath your feet. The forest floor is a tangled
mess. It is hard to find a path or even to decide which direction you are going
in. You see that the forest is choked with fallen trees and undergrowth in some
places.
name: In the Dark Forest

desc:
The forest has grown suddenly dark and oppressive. There are no animal sounds
just the cracking of twigs beneath your feet. The forest floor is a tangled
mess. It is hard to find a path or even to decide which direction you are going
in. You see that the forest is choked with fallen trees and undergrowth in some
places. You sense a looming presence adding to the darkness.
name: In the Dark Forest

desc:
The forest stretches out around you. The trees block sight of the horizon,
constraining your world to several yards before the thick foliage shuts out
sight of what lies beyond. Travel through the dense woodland is difficult, not
least due to the difficulty of choosing and following a steady course.
name: Deep in the Backwoods

desc:
Brown and coarse, the trunks of the pine trees stand needle straight, raising
their proud branches to the heavens. The evergreens cut down the light at the
forest floor level to a permanent dusk and few other plants survive in the dim
conditions. Only a few green ferns accompany the conifers.
name: Forest of Pines

desc:
A dense forest of pines grows here; their boughs are very dark green, and seem
almost black. Few indeed are the shafts of sunlight which ever pierce the thick
canopy of needles above. The forest floor is a carpet of browned needles, packed
thickly underfoot, and few are the plants which grow among the trees - some
clumps of ferns, some stunted shrubs, and some berry bushes growing in the
occasional clearing.
name: Thick Pine Forest

desc:
Here at the base of the hills to the east the ground is so thickly covered with
rotten limestone boulders that little will grow here. The trees are few, and the
stone is mostly covered with heather and with a kind of pale grey-green grass
which seems half-dead, unable to support any life. The ground is empty and
unhealthy. A few insects buzz about.
name: Stony Meadow

desc:
The forest has grown suddenly dark and oppressive. There are no animal sounds
just the cracking of twigs beneath your feet. The forest floor is a tangled
mess. It is hard to find a path or even to decide which direction you are going
in. You see that the forest is choked with fallen trees and undergrowth
undergrowth in some places.
name: In the Dark Forest

desc:
The forest has grown suddenly dark and oppressive. There are no animal sounds
mess. It is hard to find a path or even to decide which direction you are going
in. You see that the forest is choked with fallen trees and undergrowth in some
places. You sense a looming presence adding to the darkness.
name: In the Dark Forest

desc:
This small mound lies at least fifty feet higher than the surrounding area of
forest. The trees of the forest do not quite cover the exposed crest of the
mound. The break in the forest has allow thick bramble and briar bush to grow
uninhibited. The buzz of insects visiting the wild bramble berries sounds out
with the gentle rustle of wind in the trees.
name: Weather-Beaten Rise

desc:
The ground rises slightly to the west, leading up from the surrounding forest.
The pines are interspersed with birch trees, their silver bark contrasting with
the deep brown of the evergreens. A few droppings and several small burrows dug
into the slope show that you are not the only creature abroad in the woods.
name: Wooded Rise

desc:
You are in a dark, dense forest. All sounds of animals and the like have
disappeared. The light is dim here. To the east you see a large hill. Fallen
trees and thick undergrowth prevent any further northward progress.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
The large hill rises high above the forest floor. Its sides are not very steep,
but for some reason the forest has not covered the hill. Many rocks and boulders
dot the slopes and the flat hilltop.
name: On a Large Hill

desc:
The forest has grown suddenly dark and oppressive. There are no animal sounds
just the cracking of twigs beneath your feet. The forest floor is a tangled
mess. It is hard to find a clear path, or even to keep track in which direction
you are going.
name: In the Dark Forest

desc:
The forest has grown suddenly dark and oppressive. There are no animal sounds
just the cracking of twigs beneath your feet. The forest floor is a tangled
mess. It is hard to find a path or even to decide which direction you are going
in.
name: In the Dark Forest

desc:
You are standing in a light, green softwood forest with trees full of life.
There are some strange large footprints on the ground and to the east you can
see that the forest gets thinner.
name: In the Light Forest

desc:
Long slender pines let the light travel between their branches. The smell of an
old forest fills your nostrils and relaxes you. There is a large hill to the
northwest.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Long slender pines let the light travel between their branches. The smell of an
old forest fills your nostrils and relaxes you. There is a large hill to the
north.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Long slender pines let the light travel between their branches. The smell of an
old forest fills your nostrils and relaxes you. There is a large hill to the
northeast.
name: In the Forest

desc:
The woods around you grow so thickly as to make progress nigh impossible. Every
step must be won from the undergrowth as it clings to limbs and catches at feet.
The sounds of any movement through the brush echoes around the woodland before
dying away to silence once again.
name: Densely Packed Woods

desc:
The forest here has an illness upon it. The trees bear no leaves or needles and
the grass is yellowed and dry. The ground slopes up a large hillock to the north
and the forest deepens to the south and east. A strange odour curls up from the
disturbed ground under you.
name: Blighted Woodland

desc:
Long slender pines let the light travel between their branches. The smell of an
old forest fills your nostrils and relaxes you. There is a large hill to the
north.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Long slender trees let the light travel between their branches. The smell of an
old forest fills your nostrils and relaxes you.
name: In the Forest

desc:
You are standing in a light, green softwood forest with trees full of life.
There are some strange large footprints on the ground and to the east you can
see that the forest gets thinner.
name: In the Light Forest

desc:
Small outcroppings of bushes and trees grow out all around the valley. Some
small rocks are scattered about the valley floor, looking up you see the rock
formation to the south that must have dropped the stones here. The hills rise
around the valley in the south and west, and the field descends to a large
valley in the east.
name: Field between the Hills

desc:
The hills here are lower than the surrounding mounds and forest. Some trees
begin to populate the western edge of the hill leading into the Trollshaws. The
forest to the north dominates the horizon there, and not far to the south, at
the bottom of the hill, the landscape is marked by the Old East Road.
name: Hills along the Forest

desc:
The trees quickly enclose and block further passage to the south. Some light
filters through an opening in the hills to the west, and the Trollshaws continue
overhanging the lands to the east.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
The land is soft and lush here, sustaining itself from the stream to the west.
Many different types of plants grow in unorganized rows, some trampled from
wanderers roaming from the road to the south. A large group of fir trees loom
above and block the eastern lands.
name: Above the Forest

desc:
A rich mix of fir and pine trees scatter their way through the forest trail. A
thick wall of cedar trees block passage to the west and south, seemingly
impassable. Small circular stones lie on the forest floor, like eyes looking out
from the ground at the branches above.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Walls of thick cedar trees loom above you to the north and south preventing
passage. The forest edge in the west is more open, like the east. No light
filters into the passageway due to the dense canopy of leaves and limbs.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Rows of tall fir trees lead to an intersection in the middle of the Trollshaws.
The trees grow farther apart at the base but thick limbs and numerous fir
needles still manage to block out both sun and moonlight. The trees of the
forest open to all the surrounding directions here.
name: Intersection in the Trollshaws

desc:
The forest here opens slightly, the trees being higher here than anywhere else
in the Trollshaws. A wall of thick, dark trees lie to the south making that the
only route impassable to travel from this part of the forest.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Strangely a group of tall pine trees block the passage north and south, a stark
comparison to the open forest to the east and west. The forest here is rich in
vines and ivy, with some bushes growing at the base of a stand of fir trees.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
The trees give way to a small clearing here, with scattered boulders lying
circular in formation around a pit filled with bones and tattered weapons of
war. Large footprints are scattered throughout the clearing, all made by the
same type of creature. The trees allow passage to the forest in the east, west,
and south here.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
A tall stand of cedar, pine, and fir trees weave their way through the forest.
The moist canopy is overgrown by branches making the air heavy in scent from the
surrounding trees. The passage here is blocked in all directions except the
east.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Fir trees dominate the forest in this area. Thick branches and leaves block the
sky from view keeping the air thick and scented. The forest opens slightly to
the west here, and a row of trees leads east and south.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
The ground slowly rises to a ridge along the side of a tall hill. Trees line the
passage below blocking the view of what lies there. A dark overgrowth of trees
blocks your passage further to the east and south making the only route of
travel to the north or west.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
Clumps of grass grow between stones and fallen trees along the base of the
hills. The field's view of a distant horizon is blocked in all directions and
the air is stagnant due to the hills blocking any wind. The hills rise to the
east and south and a slightly higher field lies to the west.
name: Valley below the Hills

desc:
Trees clear away, broken rocks depart, and a lonely hill rises below your feet.
The grass is plentiful here, kept low to the ground by many an animal treading
through the land. The west lands open to a valley below the hills, whereas the
east continues to rise.
name: Hillock

desc:
Many hills rise among one another making a twisting passage between them that
continues to rise far to the east. The ground here is firm and makes for quick
progress among the hills. A dark forest looms above the hills to the south.
name: Rising Slope

desc:
Pine trees dominate the outer ring of the forest filling the air with a heavy
scent from their needles. Some roots grow out from the soft ground, sometimes
catching your feet. Two amply separated rows of cedars lead to the forest in the
east and south.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Cedar trees surround the ground forming a cover of leaves that prevents light
from reaching the floor. Vines cower at the forest floor hanging on the lowest
limbs, but the forest floor is clear of all other forms of life. Bones and
animal remains litter the floor creating a heavy stench.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Trees and bushes start to protrude from the ground at the base of the hill
leading to the Trollshaws to the southeast. The rising ground finally gives way
to a densely vegetated field to the east, or continue to twist and rise to the
west.
name: On the Hills

desc:
A rich mix of cedar, pine, and fir trees grow out of the rich grassland and
begin to block out the light from above. The lands to the east and west were
more unfortunate, dominated by the massive trees and the darkness created.
name: Outskirts of the Trollshaws

desc:
The grass that grows in the west quickly comes to a halt at a black shadow that
signifies the entrance of the forest. Tall cedar trees grow all around you,
their intermingling branches blocking the light from above. South and east the
forest continues, whilst to the north the foliage becomes so dense that it forms
an impenetrable natural hedge.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
You are now within the depths of the forest. The greyish, mouldy trunks of the
knotted trees seem to bend over you as you walk by, their thin, dark branches
hanging threateningly above your head. They even shift as you turn your back on
them, or so you seem to glimpse from the corner of your eye. Occasionally, a
branch drops noisily onto the rocky floor that lies hidden beneath the thick
undergrowth.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Tall trees tangle their limbs overhead, forming a solid canopy through which not
a single ray of light passes, not even on the sunniest of days. With the winds
blocked out, the air here is stale, and a dank odour of decay permeates the
area. Thin, yet thorny bushes predominate amongst the undergrowth, and they grow
with such wild proliferation that they make any attempt to progress further into
the forest quite an arduous task.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
A few dispersed trees grow all over this wide area that, unlike the rugged
terrain that is characteristic in these lands, seems rather level. Their look,
although not wholesome, is still far from resembling the sinister appearance of
the forest that looms challenging a bit westwards, if only because they stand
alone. Turning to the east, the ground seems to descend abruptly into the
lowlands, where a maze of hillocks, cracks, bluffs and gullies marks the
landscape.
name: Sparsely-wooded Terrace

desc:
The earth inclines to connect the high lands standing on the north and west to
the stony plains that stretch out into the distance towards the southeast.
Finding a path along the steep, craggy slope becomes harder and harder with
every step, and so progress is slow and straining. A few thorny bushes grow
among the rocks, littering the dull-grey, rugged landscape in brownish hues.
name: Sloping Ground

desc:
A long, deep, natural fault cuts the countryside to the south, exposing the
bowels of the earth to the light. Only a twisted, brownish brushwood clings to
the cold, grey rock. If there ever grew anything higher, it was probably swept
away by the frozen wind from the mountains, which shrouds the Bruinen valley in
its icy breath.
name: High Plains

desc:
Countless crevices of all lengths and widths run through the ground, splitting
it into patches of rock, like a stone floe breaking up little by little. And
where the hard rock has hardly endured the harsh rains, winds, and frosts, no
vegetation can grow, except a few stunted bushes with prickles all over. This
shrubbery adds a faint touch of colour to the dull, desolate moors.
name: Cracked Flatlands

desc:
A high, rocky plateau rises in the west. A large part of it seems to have come
crashing down, forming a rough hillside along which one can climb to the top,
among the sharp-edged rocks and the bare briars. When the wind blows from the
mountains, it raises a cloud of dust and pebbles, and a deep shriek, like a loud
moan, echoes throughout the plains as it slips among the rocks, as if it was
being torn apart.
name: Windswept Incline

desc:
The escarped ground here rises above the lowlands that form the valley of the
Bruinen. The descent into the plains below looks feasible, though not without
effort. An extensive, mournful-looking forest, shrouded in a deep blackness,
seems to have taken possession of most of the terrain up here. Long, twisted
branches hang defiantly from the huge, greyish trunks that mark out the
boundaries of the woodland, not far to the west.
name: Near the Eaves of a Pine Forest

desc:
Somehow the trees in this part of the forest appear to have been turned into
stone. Their barks have an ashen, lifeless look. Thick moss and lichen hang from
their trunks, exuding a penetrating smell. Even the air is dense and heavy, as
if crushed under the weight of the endless night beneath the ceiling of dark
leaves.
name: The Dark Trollshaws

desc:
If there is such a thing as the heart of this sinister-looking forest, you are
probably close now. Huge, yellowish ferns, tall as standing men, conceal most of
the ground. Nevertheless, it appears to be mostly uneven and earthy beneath your
feet, but there are also sharp, moist, slippery stones and deep hollows filled
with dark, crackling leaves.
name: Deep within the Trollshaws

desc:
The darkness in this part of the forest is so dense that the ground at your feet
seems lifeless. No plants, vines, or ivy grow here, just trees. Tall, towering
pines to short, thick cedar trees, all their leaves and branches blocking any
hope of sun or moon light. But the trees do open up enough at ground level to
allow passage in all directions.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
Vegetation in the field around you stands in ultimate contrast to the grass of
the hills above. A diverse variety of plants and colour mix together in perfect
harmony. The tranquillity of the field is broken to the south with the growth of
the deadly Trollshaws, but continues to the east.
name: Lush Field

desc:
The pine trees begin to deplete and a green meadow grows from the ground in all
directions. The air is clean and crisp, filled with the scents of the
surrounding vegetation. The Trollshaws tower over the lands to the south
seemingly impassable.
name: Meadow in the Forest

desc:
Thick plants grow in an unhealthy tangle all about you. Above your head, the
long limbs of a host of spindly evergreens knot together in an impenetrable
canopy through which no natural light seems able to pass. Dense briars form a
prickly barrier to travel north or west from here, but to the south and east the
forest seems to open up just a little, perhaps offering the possibility of
progress.
name: Trollshaws

desc:
A proliferation of pine trees grows all about you, tall, yet somehow sickly in
appearance. Nevertheless, their many twisted branches form a dense canopy
overhead, and no glimpse of the sky can be caught from beneath their leafy tops.
The perpetual gloom has spawned an undergrowth of warped and tangled briars,
among which dark, stunted ferns stand out here and there.
name: A Dark Forest

desc:
You find yourself in an elevated area with abundance of small crags and rocky
ridges. The ground is stony for the most part, with large, yellowish boulders.
Heather and bushes grow scattered among the stones, as if sprouting from the
many chinks and crevices. In the flattest areas, small groups of dark trees have
gathered, like tiny outposts of the ominous-looking forest that extends to the
west and north.
name: Clusters of Dark Trees

desc:
An extensive mesa rises above the rough plains that spread to the southeast.
Close to the north, a dark green wall marks the limits of a shadowy forest.
Strangely, no branches seem to grow outwards, as if, scared of the light, they
tried to hide in the darkness of the depths. To the east and south, the ground
descends in a series of ridges and unevenness.
name: Elevated Plains by a Dark Forest

desc:
Although the rough footing makes it difficult to notice, the ground descends
gently, but steadily towards the river that can be partially glimpsed in the
distance to the east. Somewhere behind those grey, rugged hillocks, the ford of
the Bruinen must lie. That is undoubtedly the easiest path for those trying to
reach the High Pass in the Misty Mountains, towering majestically in the
background.
name: Uneven Terrain

desc:
Standing on a steep slope of some tall and brooding hills, you see the
transition from grassy hills to lush forest. Tall oaks and pines grow high above
you, providing shade to your eyes. Quickly though, to the west and north, the
incline becomes a thick and dark forest, drawing you closer while promising only
danger.
name: Forested Incline

desc:
Row upon row of trees grow over a hundred feet high, then branch out into
countless branches all ending in leaves which fight for the sunlight unseen
above. Leaves, twigs, and even large branches lie rotting on the ground,
seemingly undisturbed by anything else. However, the occasional rustle can be
heard from...somewhere forcing you to glance hurriedly everywhere to try and
prevent an ambush.
name: The Dark Forest

desc:
Moving further into this strange and massive forest, you ponder how such a
forest could grow to such size. No smaller plants grow here, blocked out as they
are by the incredible canopy of leaves and branches overhead. The soil itself is
very rich, due to the leaves which have fallen from many feet above you. You
peer into the blackness around you and wonder where to try and move to next.
name: The Dark Forest

desc:
The trees grow as tall as ever, blocking even the tiniest ray of sunlight from
penetrating that living ceiling. The darkness has a definite edge of danger to
it. Who knows what evil might lie in these parts, away from any civilization.
The air, clammy and still, forces you to breathe more heavily. The blood
quickens with the anticipation of possible threats.
name: The Dark Forest

desc:
More and more trees stand implacably around this small glen, giving the
impression of awe before this display of nature. The majesty of the trees masks
a definitive danger, the danger of the wild. This must be home to many animals,
most of whom are not amenable to intruders. Adjusting your shield, you consider
which path to take.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The thick pine trees form an unbroken canopy above you. Their smooth unbroken
trunks rise to a great height and then spread out into thick crowns. The ground
is covered with decaying pine needles and dark stones that have a slimy
appearance. The usual fresh smell of pine needles seems to be lost in pervading
climate of darkness and decay.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The thick pine trees form an unbroken canopy above you. Their smooth unbroken
trunks rise to a great height and then spread out into thick crowns. The ground
is covered with decaying pine needles and dark stones that have a slimy
appearance. The usual fresh smell of pine needles seems to be lost in pervading
climate of darkness and decay.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The forest is still quite thin here. The trees still admit a lot of sunlight to
the forest floor where small plants grow thickly. The slope is much more gradual
here and rises gently to the north and east. To the east and north the forest
thickens rapidly.
name: In the Light Forest

desc:
You are in a hilly field climbing steeply up from the stream. Only a few trees
grow here, but you see the forest thickening rapidly within a couple of miles.
The ground is rough; full of cracks and impassable high slopes. You see the end
of the forest in the far south and hills covered with trees to the east and
north.
name: On the Hills

desc:
A shallow and narrow gully marks what must once have been the bed of a small
stream. Its waters now dried up, all that remains are a curious mixture of
stones, twigs, rotting leaves and animal remains.
name: A Dry Stream-bed

desc:
You have come out of the forest where the land falls getly eastward toward a
small, narrow gully, which might once have been a babbling stream bed.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
You are in a light, green softwood forest. Small juniper bushes grow under the
shade of the pines. The ground slopes gently eastward toward a small, narrow
gully, which might once have carried a gentle stream. To the north the trees
thicken and the land slopes upward to a huge mass of rock that rears steeply
above.
name: In the Light Forest

desc:
A small stream once flowed through the forest here, but its waters have now run
dry, leaving only a shallow gully. The trees and shrubs which used to thrive on
its waters have begun to suffer from its loss, withering and dying, and giving
way to the sturdier grasses of the open land to the east.
name: A Dry Stream-bed

desc:
You are in a grassy field. All around you you can see tree stumps some of which
show signs of being new. The deforestation seems to have been accomplished by
some animal. It is obvious that the forest once covered this entire area.
name: A Meadow

desc:
You are in a hilly field. Only a few trees grow here, but you see the forest
thickening rapidly within a couple of miles. The ground is rough, full of cracks
and impassable high slopes. You see a river in the west and the same terrain in
the north and south.
name: On the Hills

desc:
The thick pine trees form an unbroken canopy above you. Their smooth unbroken
trunks rise to a great height and then spread out into thick crowns. The ground
is covered with decaying pine needles and dark stones that have a slimy
appearance. The usual fresh smell of pine needles seems to be lost in pervading
climate of darkness and decay. The forest looks impassable to the northeast.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The thick pine trees form an unbroken canopy above you. Their smooth trunks rise
to a great height and then spread out into thick crowns. The ground is covered
with decaying pine needles and dark stones that have a slimy appearance. The
usual fresh smell of pine needles seems to be lost in pervading climate of
